feature: Inventory and dependency graph: connector SDK / cost
user_story: As an operator, I can understand and act on connector SDK cost posture without leaving Mayyam.
ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for connector SDK: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover connector SDK, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the cost pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for connector SDK.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for connector SDK.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Plugin and Action Marketplace connector SDK inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: connector-sdk_cost_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for connector SDK cost that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for connector SDK; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for connector SDK.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for connector SDK, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for connector SDK.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for connector SDK.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing connector SDK data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling connector SDK inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for connector SDK.
acceptance_criteria: Given connector SDK data exists, when the cost inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: connector SDK connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for plugin signing: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Discover plugin signing, relationships, ownership, labels, versions, and runtime dependencies for the security pillar.
backend_scope: Add collector, normalized model, repository methods, and freshness tracking for plugin signing.
frontend_scope: Add list/detail views, dependency graph, filters, and export for plugin signing.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Plugin and Action Marketplace plugin signing inventory with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: plugin-signing_security_inventory evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for plugin signing security that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for plugin signing; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for plugin signing.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for plugin signing recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for plugin signing.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for plugin signing.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing plugin signing data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling plugin signing inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for plugin signing.
acceptance_criteria: Given plugin signing data exists, when the security inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: plugin signing connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for install workflow: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Protect install workflow disaster-recovery behavior with unit, integration, fixture, and UI tests.
backend_scope: Add fixtures, fake clients, unit tests, and API contract tests for install workflow.
frontend_scope: Add component and e2e tests for install workflow critical flows.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Plugin and Action Marketplace install workflow tests with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: install-workflow_disaster-recovery_tests evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for install workflow disaster-recovery that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for install workflow; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for install workflow.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for install workflow recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for install workflow.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for install workflow.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing install workflow data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling install workflow tests broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for install workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Given install workflow data exists, when the disaster-recovery tests workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: install workflow connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for rollback workflow: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Summarize rollback workflow evidence, likely causes, confidence, uncertainty, and next diagnostic commands for performance issues.
backend_scope: Add context builder, prompt template, evidence citations, and LLM safety constraints for rollback workflow.
frontend_scope: Add triage panel, follow-up prompts, saved notes, and feedback loop for rollback workflow.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Plugin and Action Marketplace rollback workflow ai-triage with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: rollback-workflow_performance_ai-triage evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for rollback workflow performance that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for rollback workflow; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for rollback workflow.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for rollback workflow recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for rollback workflow.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for rollback workflow.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing rollback workflow data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling rollback workflow ai-triage broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for rollback workflow.
acceptance_criteria: Given rollback workflow data exists, when the performance ai-triage workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: rollback workflow connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
